data.description:
Make every rebar command emit structured JSON, and make every JSON output shape
authoritatively documented by a JSON Schema that is validated in tests across
all three interfaces (CLI, library, MCP).

## Motivation
A functionality audit found the output surface is inconsistent and undocumented:
some commands emit JSON (show/list/deps/next-batch --json), but others emit
ad-hoc text (`summary` is a line, `ready` is newline-separated ids, `list-epics`
is TSV), and NO output had a machine-readable schema — agents/clients had to
reverse-engineer shapes. A prototype now exists: a canonical JSON Schema for the
compiled ticket-state object (src/rebar/schemas/ticket_state.schema.json), a
cross-interface conformance test (tests/interfaces/test_schema_ticket_state.py),
and an advertised MCP outputSchema for show_ticket. This epic generalizes that.

## Definition of done
- Every command that returns data offers a structured-JSON form (either JSON by
  default or via a `--json` flag), with a documented, stable shape.
- Text-only outputs (`summary`, `ready`, `list-epics`, ...) gain a `--json`
  variant; the human text form may remain the default where appropriate.
- EACH such JSON output is associated with a JSON Schema under src/rebar/schemas/,
  and a test validates real output (all relevant interfaces) against it — i.e.
  "every --json instance is backed by a schema" is itself enforced by a test.
- MCP read tools advertise outputSchema (typed returns) for their structured
  outputs, consistent with the canonical schemas.
- README/CLAUDE.md/docs document the per-command output contract (link to schemas).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Inventory every command's current output (JSON / text / none) and target form
- [ ] `--json` available for every data-returning command (incl. summary/ready/list-epics)
- [ ] A JSON Schema exists under src/rebar/schemas/ for every distinct JSON output shape
- [ ] A test enforces schema coverage: every command's --json output validates against its schema, and no --json output lacks a schema (coverage guard)
- [ ] MCP read tools return typed models so outputSchema is advertised and matches the canonical schemas
- [ ] Docs updated to describe/link the output contracts
data.title: --json everywhere: structured output for every command, each backed by a JSON Schema
